fault_hypothesis: Lines 65-66 incorrectly remove the last element when both lists end with the same value, but this deduplication logic is wrong — the merged result should always contain len(left)+len(right) elements and no element should be popped.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the erroneous pop should never be true for valid inputs; this constraint is FALSE on failing tests where both lists share a last element.
After the pop branch executes, the merged list should still have len(left)+len(right) elements, which it does not because the pop incorrectly removes one element.
At the end of each merge loop iteration, the number of merged elements must equal the sum of the two pointer positions.
The returned merged list must always contain exactly len(left)+len(right) elements.
